definition.text: Vietnam is a One-Party State polity located in Southeast Asia, bordered by China, Laos, and Cambodia, with an extensive coastline along the South China Sea. It operates under a one-party system led by the Communist Party of Vietnam. Historically shaped by Chinese rule, French colonization, and the Vietnam War, the country has transitioned into a rapidly growing emerging economy with key sectors including agriculture, manufacturing, electronics, and services. Institutional development and regulatory frameworks remain evolving, with strong state involvement in strategic economic sectors.

works.title: Actor Set Evolution
works.description: Temporal mapping of Vietnam’s political and economic actors, from traditional dynasties and colonial administration to socialist state structures, Doi Moi reforms, and contemporary governance institutions.

works.title: Product Space Evolution
works.description: Network analysis of rice, coffee, electronics, and manufacturing sectors, including trade linkages within ASEAN and global markets.

works.title: Regional Spatial-Administrative Structure
works.description: Geospatial representation of provinces, major urban centers, transport infrastructure, and economic zones.
